title: cytisus supinus subsp. tmoleus
intro: Cytisus supinus subsp. tmoleus, also known as the Tmolus broom or mountain broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cytisus supinus subsp. tmoleus are thought to include antioxidant and cardiovascular protective effects, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
